source.domain: bakery, beverage, brewery, dairy, and sanitary food-processing line hardware
source.raw_idea_title: CIP Spray Ball Manifold With Tri-Clamp Branches

requirements.part_name: CIP Spray Ball Manifold With Tri-Clamp Branches - single metal part

material.reason: corrosion resistance and cleanable metal surfaces

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'CIP Spray Ball Manifold With Tri-Clamp Branches'.
The broader use case is: Sanitary cleaning loop for brewery fermenters, dairy tanks, and beverage
mix vessels. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Polished tubular manifold
with angled tri-clamp outlets and integral mounting lugs. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Distribute
cleaning solution through multiple spray devices while maintaining hygienic flow paths and removable
connections. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Challenges CAD with
repeated clamp ferrules, smooth tube intersections, angled branches, polished sanitary radii, and
readable orientation of many similar ports. The part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 185 mm x 55 mm x 44 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 9 mm on the length centerline at X=11 mm and X=174 mm, Y=27 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 61 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=92 mm, Y=27 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 46 mm x 18 mm x 3 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 37 degrees over the last 37 mm of length.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'machined angled reference face' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'CIP Spray Ball Manifold With Tri-Clamp Branches'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.raw_idea_title: Bakery Dough Chunker Infeed Guard Assembly

requirements.part_name: Bakery Dough Chunker Infeed Guard Assembly - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bakery Dough Chunker Infeed Guard Assembly'. The
broader use case is: Bakery line hardware feeding dough masses toward a divider or chunking station.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Sloped stainless infeed guard with
removable rails, hinge points, and inspection openings. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Guide sticky
dough into a cutting area while shielding operators and allowing washdown access. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests thin sheet-metal panels, sloped
product-contact surfaces, hinge hardware, guard openings, and smooth transitions that must not look
like solid blocks. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or
plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes,
slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are
bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Sanitary Butterfly Valve With Pneumatic Actuator'.
The broader use case is: Beverage, dairy, or brewery transfer line where flow must be isolated or
diverted during production and cleaning. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Tri-clamp butterfly valve body with vertical actuator, position indicator, and clamp unions. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Open and close a hygienic process line using a compact automated valve
package. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines circular valve
geometry, clamp details, actuator cylinders, shaft alignment, handle indicators, and mixed polished
and matte component surfaces. The part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using rectangular block
stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then
machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid
piece.

source.raw_idea_title: Dairy Sample Port With Sterile Cap Chain

requirements.part_name: Dairy Sample Port With Sterile Cap Chain - single metal part

material.reason: strong cylindrical metal part with threaded and slotted details

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Dairy Sample Port With Sterile Cap Chain'. The
broader use case is: Milk, yogurt, or cream processing skid requiring controlled sampling without
contaminating the main line. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Short angled
sample valve body with cap, chain, ferrule, and compact hand knob. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Provide a cleanable takeoff point for drawing small product samples from a sanitary pipe run. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Forces accurate small-part relationships:
angled nozzle, chained cap, knob grip texture, clamp interface, and clear distinction between valve
body and pipe. The part is made from 4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start from one
cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 95 mm and length 35 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 23 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 33 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 31 mm wide over 27 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M4 holes through the top flat at X=11 mm and X=23 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Brewery Whirlpool Tangential Inlet Nozzle'. The
broader use case is: Brewhouse vessel hardware for introducing wort tangentially during whirlpool
separation. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Curved or angled vessel-wall
inlet nozzle with reinforcing pad and external clamp connection. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Direct
liquid into a vessel wall with controlled tangential flow while preserving sanitary weld geometry.
It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests off-axis penetration through
a curved wall, welded reinforcement, nozzle alignment, and the visual cue that flow enters
tangentially rather than radially. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

source.raw_idea_title: Dairy Hygienic Pump Skid With Flush Base

requirements.part_name: Dairy Hygienic Pump Skid With Flush Base - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Dairy Hygienic Pump Skid With Flush Base'. The
broader use case is: Compact dairy or beverage transfer skid placed near fillers, pasteurizers, or
ingredient tanks. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Open stainless tube-
frame skid carrying pump volute, motor, sanitary elbows, and leveling feet. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Support a sanitary centrifugal pump and piping in a cleanable layout with minimal product
hold-up. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines frame tubing,
pump volute curves, motor cylinder forms, sanitary elbows, feet, and spatial clearance between
cleanable components. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar
stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the
center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or
fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 73 mm and length 42 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 14 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 24 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 34 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M7 holes through the top flat at X=14 mm and X=28 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Bakery Icing Deposit Nozzle Bar - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bakery Icing Deposit Nozzle Bar'. The broader use
case is: Bakery finishing line applying icing, glaze, or filling across moving pastries. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Horizontal sanitary header with indexed nozzle
bodies, clamp-on brackets, and adjustment knobs. All other product elements are external reference
items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Distribute viscous food
product through multiple adjustable nozzles over a conveyor path. It is intentionally included in
the SubCAD limit corpus because: Challenges regular nozzle repetition, small adjustment features,
viscous-product geometry cues, clamp brackets, and the difference between header tube, nozzle tips,
and controls. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock.
Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center,
then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are
part of the deliverable.

source.domain: bakery, beverage, brewery, dairy, and sanitary food-processing line hardware
source.raw_idea_title: Brewery Dry-Hop Dosing Hopper With Sight Window

requirements.part_name: Brewery Dry-Hop Dosing Hopper With Sight Window - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Brewery Dry-Hop Dosing Hopper With Sight Window'.
The broader use case is: Brewery ingredient handling hardware used to introduce hops or botanicals
into a closed process line. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Tapered
stainless hopper with domed lid, sight window, clamp outlet, and latch hardware. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Hold and dose loose ingredients into a sanitary transfer path while allowing operators to see
fill state. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests tapered shell
geometry, transparent insert representation, latches, sanitary outlet fittings, lid curvature, and
clear differentiation between hopper volume and external hardware. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 130 mm x 95 mm x 30 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=19 mm and X=111 mm, Y=47 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 43 mm long x 18 mm wide through the part, centered at X=65 mm, Y=47 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 32 mm x 31 mm x 5 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 34 degrees over the last 26 mm of length.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
